paragraphs.text: Implementation rules, sizing recommendations, accessibility notes and code snippets for using Phosphor Glyph Forge icons responsibly in production interfaces.
Every icon in the Forge follows three core principles: structural clarity, optical balance, and semantic restraint. These rules govern how glyphs are drawn — and how you should use them.

Icons should communicate single, unambiguous meanings. When an icon must shoulder multiple roles, pair it with a text label. Never compose two glyphs into a single signifier — instead, reach for our dedicated compound set.

All Forge icons are authored on a 24×24 base grid with 2px safe padding. Render at any of the recommended sizes below to preserve stroke crispness. Avoid arbitrary fractional scales (e.g. 19px, 27px) — they introduce sub-pixel blur.

Duotone icons combine a primary stroke layer with a secondary fill layer at 40% opacity. Maintain a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 between the primary layer and its background. Avoid placing amber glyphs on pale yellow surfaces, or coral glyphs on red backdrops.

Decorative icons should be hidden from assistive tech with aria-hidden="true". Meaningful icons require an accessible name via <title> or aria-label. Never communicate state through color alone — pair it with shape or label.
Drop the inline SVG directly into your markup. The Forge library doesn't require a runtime, web font, or sprite — every glyph is a self-contained, tree-shakeable SVG primitive.
Available weights: thin · regular · bold. Available themes: amber, coral, midnight, sage.
400+ hand-crafted duotone icons, organized by industry theme. Forged with care since 2019.

items.text:
✓ Do
            
              Use a single stroke weight per surface.
              Align glyphs to the 24px optical grid.
              Pair meaningful icons with text labels.
              Inherit

✗ Don't
            
              Mix thin and bold weights at the same size.
              Add decorative dots, ticks or sparkles.
              Recolor only part of a duotone pair.
              Em

Phosphor Glyph ForgeEST. 2019 · v4.2
      
      400+ hand-crafted duotone icons, organized by industry theme. Forged with care since 2019.

Recommended pairs
            Amber #f5a623 on charcoal #1e1b2e
            Off-white #f0ece2 on surface #2a2640
            Coral #e8436a on charcoal #1e1b2e

Avoid
            Amber on coral · coral on amber · muted #9490b8 on charcoal at sizes below 16px.
Touch targets surrounding clickable icons must be at least 44×44px.
Use the currentColor token on stroke layers to inherit text color.
Provide non-color affordances — underline, weight, position — for state changes.

visibleText:
Phosphor Glyph Forge EST. 2019 · v4.2 Browse Categories Customize Guidelines About Get Library Documentation · v4.2 Usage Guidelines & Best Practices Implementation rules, sizing recommendations, accessibility notes and code snippets for using Phosphor Glyph Forge icons responsibly in production interfaces. Section 01 Overview & Principles Every icon in the Forge follows three core principles: structural clarity , optical balance , and semantic restraint . These rules govern how glyphs are drawn — and how you should use them. Icons should communicate single, unambiguous meanings. When an icon must shoulder multiple roles, pair it with a text label. Never compose two glyphs into a single signifier — instead, reach for our dedicated compound set. Tenet 01 · Single meaning One glyph, one concept. If you can't name what it represents in two words, replace it. Section 02 Sizing & Grid Alignment All Forge icons are authored on a 24×24 base grid with 2px safe padding. Render at any of the recommended sizes below to preserve stroke crispness. Avoid arbitrary fractional scales (e.g. 19px, 27px) — they introduce sub-pixel blur. 16px 20px 24px · base 32px 48px 64px Diagram 01 · Pixel-perfect grid placement ALIGNED · 24PX GRID DRIFTED · SUB-PIXEL 0.5px offset blur Correct Avoid Section 03 Color & Contrast Duotone icons combine a primary stroke layer with a secondary fill layer at 40% opacity. Maintain a minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 between the primary layer and its background. Avoid placing amber glyphs on pale yellow surfaces, or coral glyphs on red backdrops. Diagram 02 · Concentric contrast envelopes PASS · 7:1 AAA AA primary stroke secondary fill 40% coral on charcoal ratio 5.8:1 · PASS Recommended pairs Amber #f5a623 on charcoal #1e1b2e Off-white #f0ece2 on surface #2a2640 Coral #e8436a on charcoal #1e1b2e Avoid Amber on coral · coral on amber · muted #9490b8 on charcoal at sizes below 16px. Section 04 Accessibility Decorative icons should be hidden from assistive tech with aria-hidden="true" . Meaningful icons require an accessible name via <title> or aria-label . Never communicate state through color alone — pair it with shape or label. <!-- Decorative -->
<svg aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" width="24" height="24">…</svg>

<!-- Meaningful -->
<svg role="img" aria-labelledby="t1" width="24" height="24">
  <title id="t1">Download archive</title>
  …
</svg> Touch targets surrounding clickable icons must be at least 44×44px . Use the currentColor token on stroke layers to inherit text color. Provide non-color affordances — underline, weight, position — for state changes. Section 05 Implementation Drop the inline SVG directly into your markup. The Forge library doesn't require a runtime, web font, or sprite — every glyph is a self-contained, tree-shakeable SVG primitive. import { IconAnvil } from "@phosphor/forge";

<IconAnvil
  size={24}
  weight="regular"
  color="amber"
  className="text-accent" /> Available weights: thin · regular · bold . Available themes: amber , coral , midnight , sage . Section 06 Do & Don't Diagram 03 · Visual comparison DO consistent weight · aligned DON'T mixed weights · decoration ✓ Do Use a single stroke weight per surface. Align glyphs to the 24px optical grid. Pair meaningful icons with text labels. Inherit color via currentColor . ✗ Don't Mix thin and bold weights at the same size. Add decorative dots, ticks or sparkles. Recolor only part of a duotone pair. Embed text inside the glyph viewBox. “A guideline is not a constraint — it is the shared grammar that lets a thousand glyphs feel like one voice.” — Forge Editorial Board, v4.2 On This Page Overview Sizing & Grid Color & Contrast Accessibility Implementation Do & Don't Resources ↗ Figma kit ↗ Changelog ↗ License Phosphor Glyph Forge EST. 2019 · v4.2 400+ hand-crafted duotone icons, organized by industry theme.
